tests.description: Measures throughput with disk-based buffering enabled
Verifies events survive subject restart when disk buffering is enabled
Verifies no data loss when log files are rotated by creating a new file
Verifies no data loss when log files are rotated by truncation
Measures throughput of tailing a file and forwarding over TCP
Verifies no data loss when a log file is directly truncated
Simulates a real-world pipeline with parsing, filtering, and routing
Measures throughput of a TCP-in to TCP-out pipeline with a regex mask applied to every record (e.g. CONN=<digits> → CONN=***).
Measures baseline pipeline engine overhead: add one field per record using each subject's native transform
Verifies no data loss during config reload via SIGHUP
Measures throughput of syslog message parsing in the pipeline
Measures baseline overhead of receiving TCP with no output (blackhole sink)
Measures sustained throughput of TCP-in to HTTP-out pipeline (5 minutes)
Measures throughput of TCP-in to HTTP-out pipeline
Verifies that logs sent while the HTTP receiver is down are persisted and delivered once the receiver starts
Measures sustained throughput of a TCP-in to TCP-out pipeline with no transforms (5 minutes)
Measures throughput of a TCP-in to TCP-out pipeline with no transforms
Verifies that logs sent while the receiver is down are persisted and delivered once the receiver starts
Measures throughput of a TCP-in to TCP-out pipeline with disk persistence enabled
Verifies durable persistence across a subject restart: send logs, stop subject, start receiver, restart subject, verify all logs arrive
Verifies JSON-in-string fields are parsed and re-emitted correctly
